mnemonics.title: RAT - Reflexivity Augmentation Transitivity
mnemonics.content: RAT helps remember Armstrong's Axioms: R (Reflexivity) - If Y⊆X, then X→Y; A (Augmentation) - If X→Y, then XZ→YZ; T (Transitivity) - If X→Y and Y→Z, then X→Z

mnemonics.title: Super Can Find Real Nice Girls - Superkey, Candidate Key, Normal Forms
mnemonics.content: Superkey determines all attributes; Candidate key is minimal superkey; Normal forms progress: 1NF→2NF→3NF→BCNF (each progressively 'nicer' database design)

mnemonics.title: Trivial Is Truly Tiny, Non-Trivial Is Notable
mnemonics.content: Trivial FD (X→Y where Y⊆X) is always true (tiny/insignificant); Non-trivial FD (X→Y where Y⊄X) is meaningful for normalization

mnemonics.title: A Transit Trip Across Attributes - Transitive Dependency
mnemonics.content: A→B (direct), B→C (direct), then A→C (transitive) - like taking a transit flight: A→B (flight 1), B→C (flight 2), overall A→C (trip)

mnemonics.title: Closure Calculates Completely
mnemonics.content: X+ (closure of X) calculates ALL attributes that can be determined from X using FDs - keep adding until no new attributes appear

acronyms.usage: When one attribute uniquely determines another attribute in a relation
A minimal candidate key chosen to uniquely identify tuples in a relation
A minimal set of attributes that can uniquely identify all tuples; one becomes PK
A set of attributes that can uniquely identify all attributes of a relation
Stages of database normalization based on types of dependencies present
Stronger 3NF where every FD X→Y must have X as a superkey
Minimal set of FDs equivalent to original set, no extraneous attributes

visualTips.visualization: Think of a one-way arrow: if you know X, you 'unlock' Y. Like a key (X) opening a lock (Y). Draw X→Y as X on left 'feeding' Y on right
Imagine a snowball rolling: start with X, add attributes it determines, then attributes those determine, keep growing until stable - that's X+
Superkey is a superset (bigger circle) that can have extra unnecessary attributes; Candidate key is the smallest circle that still covers everything
Chain link: A→B→C - imagine dominos falling: A knocks B, B knocks C, so A indirectly knocks C
R (Reflexivity): smaller circle inside bigger circle; A (Augmentation): add same attribute to both sides; T (Transitivity): connect two arrows to form third

analogies.analogy: Like a person's Aadhaar number (X) uniquely identifies their personal details (Y). Knowing the number reveals the details.
Like the shortest route to a destination - you can remove any intermediate stop and still reach, but remove one more and you can't get there
Like if Professor teaches Student, and Student passes Exam, then Professor indirectly determines Exam result (transitively)
Like packing a suitcase - remove all unnecessary items but keep everything essential for the trip
Like splitting a document into chapters and being able to perfectly reconstruct the original by combining them back - no information lost
